channel.title: Brisbane s calendar Light up Brisbane
channel.description: This calendar includes scheduled lights ups of Council assets including Story Bridge, Reddacliff Place sculptures, Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City Hall, King George Square, William Jolly Bridge and Tropical Dome at Brisbane Botanic Gardens Mt Coot-tha.

item.title: Eve of World Elder Abuse Awareness Day
item.description:
Sunday, 14 June 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Purple to support Eve of World Elder Abuse Awareness Day

World Elder Abuse Awareness Day (WEAAD) is a day to voice opposition to the abuse of older people. It is an annual initiative launched by the International Network for the Prevention of Elder Abuse and the World Health Organisation. WEAAD is commemorated each year on June 15 to highlight one of the worst manifestations of ageism and inequality in our society - elder abuse. What is elder abuse? Elder abuse is a single or repeated act - or lack of appropriate action - occurring within any relationship where there is an expectation of trust, which causes harm or distress to an older person. The six abuse sub-types are commonly recognised as financial abuse, physical abuse, sexual abuse, psychological abuse (otherwise known as emotional abuse), social abuse, and neglect.

This Light Up is organised by: UnitingCare Queensland Limited

Light up asset: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue address: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:summary: Eve of World Elder Abuse Awareness Day
item.xCal:description:
Victoria Bridge will be lit Purple to support Eve of World Elder Abuse Awareness Day
World Elder Abuse Awareness Day (WEAAD) is a day to voice opposition to the abuse of older people. It is an annual initiative launched by the International Network for the Prevention of Elder Abuse and the World Health Organisation. WEAAD is commemorated each year on June 15 to highlight one of the worst manifestations of ageism and inequality in our society - elder abuse. What is elder abuse? Elder abuse is a single or repeated act - or lack of appropriate action - occurring within any relationship where there is an expectation of trust, which causes harm or distress to an older person. The six abuse sub-types are commonly recognised as financial abuse, physical abuse, sexual abuse, psychological abuse (otherwise known as emotional abuse), social abuse, and neglect.
This Light Up is organised by: UnitingCare Queensland Limited

item.description:

City Hall will be lit Purple to support Eve of World Elder Abuse Awareness Day

World Elder Abuse Awareness Day (WEAAD) is a day to voice opposition to the abuse of older people. It is an annual initiative launched by the International Network for the Prevention of Elder Abuse and the World Health Organisation. WEAAD is commemorated each year on June 15 to highlight one of the worst manifestations of ageism and inequality in our society - elder abuse. What is elder abuse? Elder abuse is a single or repeated act - or lack of appropriate action - occurring within any relationship where there is an expectation of trust, which causes harm or distress to an older person. The six abuse sub-types are commonly recognised as financial abuse, physical abuse, sexual abuse, psychological abuse (otherwise known as emotional abuse), social abuse, and neglect.

This Light Up is organised by: UnitingCare Queensland Limited

Light up asset: Brisbane City Hall, Brisbane City
Venue address: Brisbane City Hall, 64 Adelaide Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Library, Light up asset

item.xCal:description:
City Hall will be lit Purple to support Eve of World Elder Abuse Awareness Day
World Elder Abuse Awareness Day (WEAAD) is a day to voice opposition to the abuse of older people. It is an annual initiative launched by the International Network for the Prevention of Elder Abuse and the World Health Organisation. WEAAD is commemorated each year on June 15 to highlight one of the worst manifestations of ageism and inequality in our society - elder abuse. What is elder abuse? Elder abuse is a single or repeated act - or lack of appropriate action - occurring within any relationship where there is an expectation of trust, which causes harm or distress to an older person. The six abuse sub-types are commonly recognised as financial abuse, physical abuse, sexual abuse, psychological abuse (otherwise known as emotional abuse), social abuse, and neglect.
This Light Up is organised by: UnitingCare Queensland Limited

item.title: Eve of World Elder Abuse Awareness Day
item.description:
Sunday, 14 June 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Purple to support Eve of World Elder Abuse Awareness Day

World Elder Abuse Awareness Day (WEAAD) is a day to voice opposition to the abuse of older people. It is an annual initiative launched by the International Network for the Prevention of Elder Abuse and the World Health Organisation. WEAAD is commemorated each year on June 15 to highlight one of the worst manifestations of ageism and inequality in our society - elder abuse. What is elder abuse? Elder abuse is a single or repeated act - or lack of appropriate action - occurring within any relationship where there is an expectation of trust, which causes harm or distress to an older person. The six abuse sub-types are commonly recognised as financial abuse, physical abuse, sexual abuse, psychological abuse (otherwise known as emotional abuse), social abuse, and neglect.

This Light Up is organised by: UnitingCare Queensland Limited

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.description:
Sunday, 14 June 2026

Story Bridge will be lit Purple to support Eve of World Elder Abuse Awareness Day

World Elder Abuse Awareness Day (WEAAD) is a day to voice opposition to the abuse of older people. It is an annual initiative launched by the International Network for the Prevention of Elder Abuse and the World Health Organisation. WEAAD is commemorated each year on June 15 to highlight one of the worst manifestations of ageism and inequality in our society - elder abuse. What is elder abuse? Elder abuse is a single or repeated act - or lack of appropriate action - occurring within any relationship where there is an expectation of trust, which causes harm or distress to an older person. The six abuse sub-types are commonly recognised as financial abuse, physical abuse, sexual abuse, psychological abuse (otherwise known as emotional abuse), social abuse, and neglect.

This Light Up is organised by: UnitingCare Queensland Limited

Light up asset: Story Bridge, Kangaroo Point

World Elder Abuse Awareness Day (WEAAD) is a day to voice opposition to the abuse of older people. It is an annual initiative launched by the International Network for the Prevention of Elder Abuse and the World Health Organisation. WEAAD is commemorated each year on June 15 to highlight one of the worst manifestations of ageism and inequality in our society - elder abuse. What is elder abuse? Elder abuse is a single or repeated act - or lack of appropriate action - occurring within any relationship where there is an expectation of trust, which causes harm or distress to an older person. World Elder Abuse Awareness Day (WEAAD) is a day to voice opposition to the abuse of older people. It is an annual initiative launched by the International Network for the Prevention of Elder Abuse and the World Health Organisation. WEAAD is commemorated each year on June 15 to highlight one of the worst manifestations of ageism and inequality in our society - elder abuse. What is elder abuse? item.title: Citizenship Ceremony
item.description:
Monday, 15 June 2026

City Hall will be lit Green and Gold to celebrate a Citizenship Ceremony 

Citizenship ceremonies are an important opportunity to welcome new citizens formally as full members of the Australian community

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane City Council

Light up asset: Brisbane City Hall, Brisbane City
Venue address: Brisbane City Hall, 64 Adelaide Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Library, Light up asset
item.x-trumba:customfield: Brisbane City Hall, 64 Adelaide Street, Brisbane City

item.title: MND Global Awareness Day
item.description:
Monday, 15 June 2026

Reddacliff Place sculptures will be lit Blue to support MND Global Awareness Day

MND Global Awareness Day is an opportunity to raise vital funds and awareness to support those living with Motor Neurone Disease

This Light Up is organised by: MND Queensland

Light up asset: Reddacliff Place, Brisbane City
Venue address: Reddacliff Place, 266 George Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
Reddacliff Place sculptures will be lit Blue to support MND Global Awareness Day
MND Global Awareness Day is an opportunity to raise vital funds and awareness to support those living with Motor Neurone Disease
This Light Up is organised by: MND Queensland
item.x-trumba:customfield: Reddacliff Place, 266 George Street, Brisbane City

item.title: Go Green For Ecomarines
item.description:
Tuesday, 16 June 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Green to support Go Green For Ecomarines

Go Green for EcoMarines is a month-long campaign to raise awareness and funds to support an education-based action program targeting a reduction in waste and pollution and promoting recycling and other sustainable living options. EcoMarines Foundation started in 2014 with just six schools, and we are now in over 300 schools and Early Learning Centres across Queensland

This Light Up is organised by: Ecomarines Foundation

Light up asset: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue address: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
Victoria Bridge will be lit Green to support Go Green For Ecomarines
Go Green for EcoMarines is a month-long campaign to raise awareness and funds to support an education-based action program targeting a reduction in waste and pollution and promoting recycling and other sustainable living options. EcoMarines Foundation started in 2014 with just six schools, and we are now in over 300 schools and Early Learning Centres across Queensland
This Light Up is organised by: Ecomarines Foundation

item.title: State of Origin - Game 2 Melbourne
item.description:
Wednesday, 17 June 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Maroon to celebrate State of Origin - Game 2 Melbourne

Experience the all-consuming power of Australia’s greatest sporting rivalry as Origin returns for 2026 with Game II at the mighty MCG, Melbourne | Wednesday, 17 June
Get ready to witness an unmatched display of intensity as the game’s best players square off in the ultimate fight for state pride. The only thing that compares to the intensity, ferocity and rivalry on the field, is the all-consuming rivalry that sweeps the fans in the stands.
Ampol State of Origin.
Nothing like it.

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane Economic Development Agency

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Maroon to celebrate State of Origin - Game 2 Melbourne
Experience the all-consuming power of Australia’s greatest sporting rivalry as Origin returns for 2026 with Game II at the mighty MCG, Melbourne | Wednesday, 17 June Get ready to witness an unmatched display of intensity as the game’s best players square off in the ultimate fight for state pride. The only thing that compares to the intensity, ferocity and rivalry on the field, is the all-consuming rivalry that sweeps the fans in the stands. Ampol State of Origin. Nothing like it.
This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane Economic Development Agency
item.x-trumba:customfield: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point

item.title: Wear Your Dangerous Females T-Shirt Day
item.description:
Thursday, 18 June 2026

Story Bridge will be lit Purple and Pink to support Wear Your Dangerous Females T-Shirt Day
 
Wear Your Dangerous Females T-Shirt Day is about turning everyday spaces into opportunities for conversation about domestic and family violence. Every Dangerous Females t-shirt is designed to challenge stigma and help fund awareness initiatives and support projects for women and families impacted by violence. This day invites folks to wear their tee in the workplace, a place where they might normally start these conversations.
Dangerous Females is a Brisbane not-for-profit organisation raising awareness about domestic and family violence through, community campaigns, fundraising events, and artist collaborations. We work to make advocacy accessible, using fashion, storytelling, and grassroots action to engage people who might not otherwise connect with the issue. We donate 100% profits from our tees to DV services and are celebrating donating half-a-million since we started in 2018.
 
This Light Up is organised by: Dangerous Females

Light up asset: Story Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Story Bridge, Main Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:summary: Wear Your Dangerous Females T-Shirt Day
item.x-trumba:customfield: Story Bridge, Main Street, Kangaroo Point

item.title: Wear Your Dangerous Females T-Shirt Day
item.description:
Thursday, 18 June 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Purple and Pink to support Wear Your Dangerous Females T-Shirt Day
 
Wear Your Dangerous Females T-Shirt Day is about turning everyday spaces into opportunities for conversation about domestic and family violence. Every Dangerous Females t-shirt is designed to challenge stigma and help fund awareness initiatives and support projects for women and families impacted by violence. This day invites folks to wear their tee in the workplace, a place where they might normally start these conversations.
Dangerous Females is a Brisbane not-for-profit organisation raising awareness about domestic and family violence through, community campaigns, fundraising events, and artist collaborations. We work to make advocacy accessible, using fashion, storytelling, and grassroots action to engage people who might not otherwise connect with the issue. We donate 100% profits from our tees to DV services and are celebrating donating half-a-million since we started in 2018.
 
This Light Up is organised by: Dangerous Females

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:summary: Wear Your Dangerous Females T-Shirt Day
item.x-trumba:customfield: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point

item.title: World Sickle Cell Day
item.description:
Friday, 19 June 2026

City Hall will be lit Red to support World Sickle Cell Day

World Sickle Cell Day, which takes place on June 19 each year, is an internationally recognised awareness day promoting equitable access to treatment of those living with sickle cell anaemia and to break down stigma in the community. Lighting landmarks in red help’s raise public awareness of Sickle Cell, sparks conversation, and increases understanding and acknowledge the experiences of individuals and families affected.

This Light Up is organised by: Thalassaemia and Sickle Cell Australia

Light up asset: Brisbane City Hall, Brisbane City
Venue address: Brisbane City Hall, 64 Adelaide Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Library, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
City Hall will be lit Red to support World Sickle Cell Day
World Sickle Cell Day, which takes place on June 19 each year, is an internationally recognised awareness day promoting equitable access to treatment of those living with sickle cell anaemia and to break down stigma in the community. Lighting landmarks in red help’s raise public awareness of Sickle Cell, sparks conversation, and increases understanding and acknowledge the experiences of individuals and families affected.
This Light Up is organised by: Thalassaemia and Sickle Cell Australia
item.x-trumba:customfield: Brisbane City Hall, 64 Adelaide Street, Brisbane City

item.title: World Sickle Cell Day
item.description:
Friday, 19 June 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Red to support World Sickle Cell Day

World Sickle Cell Day, which takes place on June 19 each year, is an internationally recognised awareness day promoting equitable access to treatment of those living with sickle cell anaemia and to break down stigma in the community. Lighting landmarks in red help’s raise public awareness of Sickle Cell, sparks conversation, and increases understanding and acknowledge the experiences of individuals and families affected.

This Light Up is organised by: Thalassaemia and Sickle Cell Australia
item.xCal:description:
Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Red to support World Sickle Cell Day
World Sickle Cell Day, which takes place on June 19 each year, is an internationally recognised awareness day promoting equitable access to treatment of those living with sickle cell anaemia and to break down stigma in the community. Lighting landmarks in red help’s raise public awareness of Sickle Cell, sparks conversation, and increases understanding and acknowledge the experiences of individuals and families affected.
This Light Up is organised by: Thalassaemia and Sickle Cell Australia

World Sickle Cell Day, which takes place on June 19 each year, is an internationally recognised awareness day promoting equitable access to treatment of those living with sickle cell anaemia and to break down stigma in the community. Lighting landmarks in red help’s raise public awareness of Sickle Cell, sparks conversation, and increases understanding and acknowledge the experiences of individuals and families affected.

World Sickle Cell Day, which takes place on June 19 each year, is an internationally recognised awareness day promoting equitable access to treatment of those living with sickle cell anaemia and to break down stigma in the community. Lighting landmarks in red help’s raise public awareness of Sickle Cell, sparks conversation, and increases understanding and acknowledge the experiences of individuals and families affected.

World Sickle Cell Day, which takes place on June 19 each year, is an internationally recognised awareness day promoting equitable access to treatment of those living with sickle cell anaemia and to break down stigma in the community. item.title: Migraine Awareness Month/Shades for Migraine Day
item.description:
Sunday, 21 June 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Purple to support Migraine Awareness Month/Shades for Migraine Day

Shades for Migraine - Sunday, June 21: Wear sunglasses to symbolize sensitivity and post a selfie with the hashtag #ShadesForMigraine.
Migraine Australia is the national patient advocacy organisation for people living with migraine, their carers and families. We strive to be the leading source of information about migraine, collaborating with all stakeholders on behalf of people impacted by migraine. Led by people who have lived experience with migraine or who care for those who do. They are encouraged to join the our online support groups, website and social media spaces to keep informed.

This Light Up is organised by: Migraine Australia Ltd

Light up asset: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue address: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:summary: Migraine Awareness Month/Shades for Migraine Day
item.xCal:description:
Victoria Bridge will be lit Purple to support Migraine Awareness Month/Shades for Migraine Day
Shades for Migraine - Sunday, June 21: Wear sunglasses to symbolize sensitivity and post a selfie with the hashtag #ShadesForMigraine. Migraine Australia is the national patient advocacy organisation for people living with migraine, their carers and families. We strive to be the leading source of information about migraine, collaborating with all stakeholders on behalf of people impacted by migraine. Led by people who have lived experience with migraine or who care for those who do. They are encouraged to join the our online support groups, website and social media spaces to keep informed.
This Light Up is organised by: Migraine Australia Ltd

item.title: The 65th Annual Queen's Ball
item.description:
Sunday, 21 June 2026

City Hall will be lit Rainbow to celebrate The 65th Annual Queen's Ball

The Queens Ball is a flagship annual event that honours outstanding contributions to the LGBTIQA+ community while raising critical funds to support ongoing programs, grants, and advocacy work. It brings together community members, leaders, and allies in a formal setting that fosters connection, visibility, and shared identity, while also reinforcing the community's values and recognising its history and progress

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane Pride

Light up asset: Brisbane City Hall, Brisbane City
Venue address: Brisbane City Hall, 64 Adelaide Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Library, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
City Hall will be lit Rainbow to celebrate The 65th Annual Queen's Ball
The Queens Ball is a flagship annual event that honours outstanding contributions to the LGBTIQA+ community while raising critical funds to support ongoing programs, grants, and advocacy work. It brings together community members, leaders, and allies in a formal setting that fosters connection, visibility, and shared identity, while also reinforcing the community's values and recognising its history and progress
This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane Pride
item.x-trumba:customfield: Brisbane City Hall, 64 Adelaide Street, Brisbane City

item.title: Routine Testing
item.description:
Monday, 22 June 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge decorative lighting will undergo routine testing on Monday 22 June 2026, between 17:00 and 20:00.

During this time, the Bridge will change through multiple colours and patterns before returning to the City of Brisbane colours of blue and gold once complete.

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane City Council

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
Kangaroo Point Bridge decorative lighting will undergo routine testing on Monday 22 June 2026, between 17:00 and 20:00.
During this time, the Bridge will change through multiple colours and patterns before returning to the City of Brisbane colours of blue and gold once complete.
This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane City Council
item.x-trumba:customfield: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point

item.title: Olympic Day
item.description:
Tuesday, 23 June 2026

City Hall will be lit Olympic Colours to celebrate Olympic Day

Olympic Day is celebrated all around the world on 23 June, in global celebration of the founding of the International Olympic Committee and the modern Olympic Movement in 1894.

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane City Council

Light up asset: Brisbane City Hall, Brisbane City
Venue address: Brisbane City Hall, 64 Adelaide Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Library, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
City Hall will be lit Olympic Colours to celebrate Olympic Day
Olympic Day is celebrated all around the world on 23 June, in global celebration of the founding of the International Olympic Committee and the modern Olympic Movement in 1894.
This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane City Council
item.x-trumba:customfield: Brisbane City Hall, 64 Adelaide Street, Brisbane City

item.title: 35th Anniversary of Slovenian Independence
item.description:
Wednesday, 24 June 2026

Story Bridge will be lit White, Blue and Red to celebrate the 35th Anniversary of Slovenian Independence


The 35th Anniversary of Slovenian Independence celebrates the strong relationship between Slovenia and Queensland, and promotes cultural understanding and goodwill among members of the diplomatic, business, and wider community. The event will provide an opportunity to recognise Slovenia's achievements as an independent nation and strengthen connections between the Slovenian and Queensland communities.


This Light Up is organised by: The Embassy of Slovenia
item.xCal:description:
Story Bridge will be lit White, Blue and Red to celebrate the 35th Anniversary of Slovenian Independence
The 35th Anniversary of Slovenian Independence celebrates the strong relationship between Slovenia and Queensland, and promotes cultural understanding and goodwill among members of the diplomatic, business, and wider community. The event will provide an opportunity to recognise Slovenia's achievements as an independent nation and strengthen connections between the Slovenian and Queensland communities.
This Light Up is organised by: The Embassy of Slovenia

item.title: PTSD Awareness Day
item.description:
Saturday, 27 June 2026

Story Bridge will be lit Teal to support PTSD Awareness Day

Post-traumatic Stress Disorder Awareness Day is to show the million-plus Australians living with the condition that they are not alone. By illuminating iconic landmarks in teal blue, we aim to spark vital community conversations, reduce stigma, and guide those affected toward effective, life-changing support. It serves as a timely reminder of the mental health impacts of adversity, from everyday trauma to environmental disasters like bushfires and floods.

This Light Up is organised by: Phoenix Australia

Light up asset: Story Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Story Bridge, Main Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
Story Bridge will be lit Teal to support PTSD Awareness Day
Post-traumatic Stress Disorder Awareness Day is to show the million-plus Australians living with the condition that they are not alone. By illuminating iconic landmarks in teal blue, we aim to spark vital community conversations, reduce stigma, and guide those affected toward effective, life-changing support. It serves as a timely reminder of the mental health impacts of adversity, from everyday trauma to environmental disasters like bushfires and floods.
This Light Up is organised by: Phoenix Australia
item.x-trumba:customfield: Story Bridge, Main Street, Kangaroo Point
